---
title: "SemesterView — Product Specification"
date: "2026-03-10"
---

**Version:** 1.0 · **Status:** MVP shipped · **Live URL:** [semesterview-app.vercel.app](https://semesterview-app.vercel.app)

---

## 1. Product Overview

SemesterView is a semester-long calendar tool for college students that surfaces scheduling conflicts before they happen. Unlike week-by-week planners, it shows the entire semester at once — layering recurring commitments (classes, work shifts, meetings) on top of variable obligations (exams, deadlines, personal events) so students can spot collisions early.

**Core problem solved:** College students with planning habits still drop the ball because their tools only show them the near future. By the time they see a conflict, it's already a crisis.

**The one core action:** A user opens the app, sees their full semester, and immediately identifies a week where two high-stakes obligations collide — before either has passed.

---

## 2. Target User

**Persona: Emma Chen**

| Attribute | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| Age / Role | 20–22, junior or senior undergraduate |
| University | Mid-to-large (3,000+ students) |
| Course load | 15+ credit hours + 2–3 external commitments |
| Pain moment | Sunday night planning and mid-semester crunch |
| Tools today | Color-coded planners, Canvas, phone reminders, sticky notes |

**What Emma wants:**

- Good grades without sacrificing relationships, health, or personal goals
- To feel in control — not constantly reacting to the next thing
- To stop losing 3–5 hours/week to last-minute scrambles

**Willingness to pay:** Moderate (3/5). Price-sensitive personally; parents may subsidize if the product demonstrably reduces stress and improves academic outcomes.

---

## 3. Value Proposition

> We help college students who have a planning system but still drop the ball **see conflicts before they happen** by showing the entire semester at once and flagging when academic obligations collide with important personal and family events.

**Differentiation:** Not another to-do list or week view. The insight is the full-semester horizon — the thing no current tool gives students without manual effort.

---

## 4. Features As Built

### 4.1 Authentication & Data Persistence

- Email/password signup and login via Supabase Auth
- All event data stored server-side, scoped to the authenticated user via Row Level Security (RLS)
- Sessions persist for 30 days without re-login (cookie-based via `@supabase/ssr`)
- Logout destroys the session and redirects to login

### 4.2 Semester Setup & Switching

- On first login, users are prompted to create a semester (name, start date, end date)
- Multiple semesters supported — users can create additional semesters and switch between them via a dropdown in the header
- Active semester persisted in `localStorage` so the correct semester loads on return visits

### 4.3 Recurring Commitments

Recurring commitments represent fixed, repeating events (classes, work shifts, weekly meetings).

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| title | string | yes | |
| days_of_week | string[] | yes | e.g. `["monday", "wednesday"]` |
| start_time | string | yes | 24h format HH:MM |
| end_time | string | yes | Must be after start_time |
| start_date | date | yes | Must be within semester range |
| end_date | date | yes | Must be after start_date |
| category | enum | yes | `school`, `work`, `other` |

**Visual styling:** Cool colors — school (blue), work (green), other (gray).

### 4.4 Variable Obligations

Variable obligations represent one-time, high-stakes events (exams, deadlines, personal events).

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| title | string | yes | |
| date | date | yes | Must be within semester range |
| time | string | no | 24h format HH:MM |
| category | enum | yes | `exam`, `deadline`, `personal`, `family`, `work` |

**Visual styling:** Warm colors — exam (red/pink), deadline (orange), personal (purple), family (yellow), work (green).

**Delete behaviors:**

- **Delete this obligation** — removes the single event permanently from the database
- **Delete All Future Events of this Title** — hides all obligations with the same title client-side via `localStorage`; events remain in the database but are filtered from the UI

### 4.5 Conflict Detection

Conflict detection is computed entirely client-side on every render, wrapped in `useMemo`.

| Type | Trigger |
|------|---------|
| High-priority cluster | 3+ events with category `exam`, `deadline`, `personal`, or `family` on the same day |
| Time overlap | Any two events with an exact start/end time overlap on the same day |

Flags render inline in the semester grid without any user action and update in real time when events are added, edited, or deleted.

### 4.6 Calendar Import

Users can import events from external calendars via `.ics` or `.ical` files.

**Supported sources:**

- **Canvas** — Calendar → Calendar Feed (bottom right) → Click to View Calendar Feed → download
- **Learning Suite** — Open a course → Schedule → Get iCalendar Feed → Download Calendar File
- **Google Calendar** — Settings gear → Import & Export → Export → download (unzip if multiple calendars)

**Import behavior:**

- Parsed client-side with a custom RFC 5545 iCal parser (no third-party dependency)
- Category auto-inferred: titles containing exam/quiz/test/midterm/final → `exam`, all others → `deadline`
- User can review and override category per event before confirming
- Up to 500 events per import batch
- **One-time snapshot** — imports do not sync; re-import required to pull in new events

---

## 5. Data Model

### Semesters

```sql
CREATE TABLE semesters (
  id          uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id     uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name        text NOT NULL,
  start_date  date NOT NULL,
  end_date    date NOT NULL,
  created_at  timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
```

### Recurring Commitments

```sql
CREATE TYPE commitment_category AS ENUM ('school', 'work', 'other');
CREATE TYPE day_of_week AS ENUM ('monday','tuesday','wednesday','thursday','friday','saturday','sunday');

CREATE TABLE recurring_commitments (
  id           uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id      uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  semester_id  uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES semesters(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  title        text NOT NULL,
  days_of_week day_of_week[] NOT NULL,
  start_time   text NOT NULL,
  end_time     text NOT NULL,
  start_date   date NOT NULL,
  end_date     date NOT NULL,
  category     commitment_category NOT NULL DEFAULT 'school',
  created_at   timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
```

### Variable Obligations

```sql
CREATE TYPE obligation_category AS ENUM ('exam','deadline','personal','family','work');

CREATE TABLE variable_obligations (
  id           uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id      uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  semester_id  uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES semesters(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  title        text NOT NULL,
  date         date NOT NULL,
  time         text,
  category     obligation_category NOT NULL,
  created_at   timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
```

All tables have Row Level Security enabled — users can only read and write their own rows.

---

## 6. API Reference

All endpoints require an authenticated Supabase session. Unauthenticated requests return `401`.

### Semesters

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/semesters` | List all semesters for the authenticated user |
| POST | `/api/semesters` | Create a new semester |

### Recurring Commitments

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/recurring-commitments?semester_id={id}` | List commitments for a semester |
| POST | `/api/recurring-commitments` | Create a new commitment |
| PUT | `/api/recurring-commitments/[id]` | Update a commitment |
| DELETE | `/api/recurring-commitments/[id]` | Delete a commitment |

### Variable Obligations

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/variable-obligations?semester_id={id}` | List obligations for a semester |
| POST | `/api/variable-obligations` | Create a new obligation |
| PUT | `/api/variable-obligations/[id]` | Update an obligation |
| DELETE | `/api/variable-obligations/[id]` | Delete an obligation |

### Import

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/import/ical` | Bulk insert up to 500 obligations from a parsed iCal file |

---

## 7. Technical Architecture

### Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router, Turbopack) |
| Language | TypeScript |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 |
| UI components | shadcn/ui v3 |
| Forms | react-hook-form + Zod v4 |
| Auth | Supabase SSR |
| Database | Supabase PostgreSQL |
| Hosting | Vercel |

### State Management

All server state is managed by a custom hook (`useSemesterData`) that applies optimistic updates — UI changes reflect immediately before API confirmation, with rollback on failure. Conflict detection is pure client-side, running on every render via `useMemo` with no server calls required.

---

## 8. UI/UX Decisions

### Color System

| Category | Use |
|----------|-----|
| School (blue) | Recurring classes |
| Work (green) | Recurring work shifts |
| Other (gray) | Other recurring events |
| Exam (red/pink) | High-stakes test dates |
| Deadline (orange) | Assignment due dates |
| Personal (purple) | Personal events |
| Family (yellow) | Family events |

### Interaction Patterns

- **Optimistic updates** — every create/edit/delete reflects in the UI immediately, with silent rollback on API failure
- **Slide-in sheet** — event creation and editing uses a right-side sheet to keep the semester view visible
- **Confirmation dialogs** — destructive actions require explicit confirmation
- **Inline validation** — all form errors surface at the field level on blur or submit attempt

---

## 9. Known Limitations

| Limitation | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| iCal import is one-time | No live sync — re-import required for new events |
| Hide vs. delete | "Delete All Future Events" hides events in localStorage rather than deleting from the database |
| No recurring event import | iCal RRULE events are not parsed — each instance is imported as a separate variable obligation |
| No undo | Deletion is permanent |
| Obligation categories only | Imported events cannot be created as recurring commitments |

---

## 10. Out of Scope (Post-MVP)

| Feature | Notes |
|---------|-------|
| Canvas / LMS OAuth | Live sync via API — requires institution-by-institution OAuth setup |
| Google Calendar live sync | Would require Google OAuth and webhooks |
| Convert obligation to commitment | Requires user to supply missing recurring fields |
| Push / email notifications | Reminders for upcoming conflict weeks |
| Sharing a semester view | Read-only shareable link |
| Dark mode | Not implemented |
| Undo / redo | Not implemented |

---

## 11. Success Metrics

| Function | Metric | Target |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Auth & Persistence | % of users who create an account and find events intact after logout/login | 100% |
| Event Entry | % of users who successfully create both event types without assistance | >90% |
| Semester View | % of users who can describe a flagged week without clicking into it | >80% |
| Conflict Detection | % of users who identify a known conflict using the app | 100% |
